instruction:
Based on the sample data, the 90\% confidence interval limits for the population mean are LCL=170.86, UCL=195.42. If the 10\% level of significance were used in testing the hypotheses $H_0: \mu=201$ vs. $H_1: \mu \not=201$, the null hypothesis: [ANS] A. would be rejected  B. would have to be revised  C. would not be rejected  D. None of the above
A random sample of 25 observations is selected from a normally distributed population. The sample variance is 10. In the 95\% confidence interval for the population variance, the upper limit will be: [ANS] A. 17.331  B. 17.110  C. 19.353  D. 6.097
